howItWorks.intro: This customs duty calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Customs Value (CIF) = Goods Value + Shipping + Insurance
Merchandise Processing Fee = 0.3464% of value (min $31.67, max $614.35)
Total Landed Cost = CIF + Duty + MPF
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How are import duties calculated?
faq.answer: US Customs assesses duty on the CIF value (Cost + Insurance + Freight). The duty rate depends on the Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) code for your product and the country of origin. Rates range from 0% to 37.5%+.

faq.question: Is there a duty-free threshold?
faq.answer: Yes. US Section 321 allows imports valued under $800 to enter duty-free for personal shipments. This is the 'de minimis' threshold. Commercial shipments have different rules and may still require formal entry.

faq.question: What is the Merchandise Processing Fee?
faq.answer: The MPF is a fee charged on all formal entries (goods over $2,500). It's 0.3464% of the customs value, with a minimum of $31.67 and maximum of $614.35 per entry. Informal entries pay a flat $2-9 fee.
